message:
feat(planner): add AccessPathProbe::InList for indexed IN-list seeks

Extends the AccessPathProbe enum so the planner can describe an index
scan driven by `WHERE <leading_col> IN (<v1>, <v2>, ...)` as a
first-class probe kind:

  AccessPathProbe::InList {
      column: String,
      values: Vec<Box<Expr>>,
  }

extract_access_path_probe gets a second lookup pass: after failing to
find an equality term on the leading index column, it now looks for
an InList term and hands off to the new extract_in_list_probe helper.
The helper unwraps Expr::In { set: InSet::List(items), not: false },
clones the items into boxed expressions, and returns None for empty
lists (caller falls back to full scan).

Equality probes still take precedence — added
extract_probe_in_list_prefers_equality_over_in to lock that ordering
down so a future refactor can't regress planner choice for a hot
equality lookup masked by a parallel IN clause.

The downstream executor will later interpret InList as "one index
seek per value" which is the right shape when values.len() × seek_cost
< full_scan_cost. Cost estimation is not changed here — that lives in
its own follow-up — so the planner currently only picks InList when
the AccessPathKind is already IndexScanEquality. Behavioural delta is
zero until the costing layer starts consulting the new variant.
